title: Plans for Temporary Fire Station in Heywood Advance
excerpt: Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service is moving forward with plans for a temporary fire station in Heywood while the existing facility is rebuilt.
content_html:
Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service (GMFRS) is set to construct a temporary fire station in Heywood as part of a redevelopment project for the existing site.

The new station will be located behind the current facility on Middleton Road, which is approximately 60 years old and deemed in need of replacement.

The temporary structure will be a two-storey modular building designed to accommodate one fire engine, while an existing outbuilding will house additional specialist vehicles.

This initiative is part of a broader £38 million Estates Strategy aimed at modernising fire stations across Greater Manchester to enhance service delivery.

Planning documents indicate that the temporary station will maintain emergency response capabilities during the redevelopment, ensuring uninterrupted service for the community.

A separate planning application for the demolition of the old station and the construction of a new community fire station, which will feature improved facilities, is expected to be submitted soon.

title: Safety Tips for Using Electric Fans During Heatwave
excerpt: As temperatures soar across the UK, health experts are advising residents on the safe use of electric fans to prevent overheating and electrical hazards.
content_html:
With the ongoing heatwave affecting much of the UK, health and safety experts are emphasising the importance of using electric fans safely. Many households are relying on these appliances to stay cool, but precautions are necessary to avoid potential risks.

High Speed Training, a health and safety training provider, has shared essential guidance for fan usage. Dr Richard Anderson, the organisation's Director of Learning, highlights that while fans can help alleviate heat, they must be used correctly to ensure safety.

Before using a fan, individuals should inspect it for any signs of damage, particularly checking the plug and cable. If a fan emits unusual sounds or if the plug feels warm, it should be turned off immediately and examined by a qualified electrician.

Cleaning dust from fan blades is also recommended to reduce motor strain and the risk of overheating. Fans should be unplugged during cleaning, and users should follow the manufacturer's instructions when reassembling the device.

Experts advise against leaving fans running unattended or while sleeping. Instead, it is suggested to cool down bedrooms before bedtime by operating the fan with windows open when suitable. Proper positioning of the fan on a stable surface, away from curtains or obstructions, is crucial for safe operation.

Dr Anderson reiterates that while fans are an effective and economical way to combat heat, they are still electrical devices that require careful handling. Simple safety checks can prevent accidents and ensure the fan operates efficiently.

title: Plans for New SEND School on Kingsway West Business Park
excerpt: A proposal to convert a vacant warehouse into a school for children with special educational needs and disabilities has been submitted by Whitehall Academy.
content_html:
A new educational facility catering to children with special educational needs and disabilities (SEND) is being proposed for a vacant warehouse located at Kingsway West Business Park in Rochdale.

The plans, submitted by Whitehall Academy, aim to transform the empty unit into a school accommodating up to 50 pupils. The design includes six classrooms, three sensory rooms, a multi-purpose hall, and various support spaces such as offices and a medical room.

The exterior of the building will remain unchanged, but a secure play area with artificial grass will be created on the west side, replacing previously unused space.

This new school would serve as an extension of Whitehall Academy's existing premises on Whitehall Street, which is a specialist independent primary school focused on supporting vulnerable children with complex needs.

Students at the proposed school may have diagnoses including autism and learning difficulties, and all will have an Education, Health, and Care Plan (EHCP) or be in the assessment process.

The school is expected to operate from 9am to 3pm for pupils, with staff hours extending from 8am to 4pm, potentially including breakfast and after-school clubs that could extend the day until 5pm.

The warehouse was previously occupied by Circuitmaster Designs Ltd, an engineering firm, and has been vacant since October 2024.

title: Man 'deliberately' drove at teenage e-bike thief in Middleton
excerpt: A man is on trial accused of murdering a 15-year-old boy in an act of revenge.
content_html:
A man is on trial accused of murdering a 15-year-old boy in an act of revenge.

Devon Simmonds-Caines, 15, died in Middleton in July 2025 A man deliberately drove his car at a 15-year-old boy who had stolen an e-bike in an act of revenge, a trial has heard. It has been alleged that Terrence King intentionally struck Devon Simmonds-Caines on Rowrah Crescent in Middleton, Greater Manchester, on 22 July 2025. Michael Hayton KC, outlining the prosecution's case at Manchester's Minshull Street Crown Court, said it "was not a tragic motor accident or poor driving". The jury was told King, 60, had admitted manslaughter and accepted his actions caused Devon's death, but he denied murder. CCTV and doorbell camera footage showed Simmonds-Caines riding an e-bike, which the court heard he had stolen from another boy. King's car was seen driving at speed on the road before veering left, mounting the pavement and crashing into Simmonds-Caines, who died at the scene from his injuries. The footage was too upsetting for some of the boy's family, who left the court in tears.

A man from Rochdale has been jailed following a burglary which saw him steal a car in Warrington earlier this year. Adam Lamb, 23, appeared at Liverpool Crown Court on Friday (July 3) after pleading guilty to dwelling burglary and taking a vehicle without consent. In the early hours of February 6, Lamb and another man broke into a home on Dam Lane, stealing keys to a BMW parked outside. The pair drove off in the stolen vehicle, using it to force another car off the driveway. The theft was captured on a mobile phone by the vehicle’s owner, who contacted Cheshire Police and informed officers that the BMW was fitted with a tracker and heading towards Bolton. title: Metrolink Lines to Close for Three Weeks Due to Upgrade Works
excerpt: Significant disruptions to tram services are set to occur as part of a £150m upgrade, affecting multiple lines in Greater Manchester.
content_html:
Transport for Greater Manchester (TfGM) has announced that three Metrolink lines will be closed for three weeks starting from 13 July as part of a major £150m upgrade project.

The affected lines include the Eccles, Trafford Centre, and Altrincham routes, with no trams operating during this period. Services on the East Didsbury and Manchester Airport lines will also be impacted, terminating at Firswood instead of their usual destinations.

In addition, the Rochdale line will terminate at Exchange Square during the upgrade. Replacement bus services will be provided to assist passengers between the affected stops.

The upgrade work involves track replacement between Trafford Bar, Cornbrook, and Deansgate-Castlefield, which is known to be one of the busiest tram stretches in the country. TfGM aims to minimise future disruptions by conducting additional work at Eccles and around Piccadilly Gardens during this closure.

A special bus service will operate for The Hundred event at Old Trafford Cricket Ground on 26 and 31 July, ensuring fans can still access the venue despite the tram disruptions.

The academy began with a single Year 7 cohort and has since grown, with students contributing to the establishment of the school's culture and traditions over the past five years. The inspection also acknowledged the academy's commitment to student wellbeing, noting that children feel listened to and valued. The report highlighted the school's initiatives to enhance literacy and numeracy, as well as support for students facing learning barriers.

Richard Ronksley, CEO of Altus Education Partnership, remarked on the dedication of staff, families, and students in building the school from its inception. He praised the resilience and ambition demonstrated by the students, marking this achievement as a reflection of their collective efforts.

At 68 years old, Nuttall, a retired HR administrator, plays a crucial role in the group, focusing on grants and fundraising efforts. Her background in bookkeeping and financial management has been instrumental in securing funding for various projects aimed at enhancing the park.

excerpt: Joseph Holt has introduced a £1 children's meal deal across its pubs, aiming to support families during the summer holidays. The brewery has also initiated a fundraising campaign to raise £20,000 for the charity Meals & More, which addresses holiday hunger in the North West.
content_html:
Joseph Holt Brewery has announced a special summer initiative, offering children's meals for just £1 at its pubs, including locations in Bury. This promotion is designed to assist families during the school holidays and runs from 25 June to 1 September.

The brewery, which operates 127 pubs across the North West, has reduced the price of children's meals by over 60%. Customers can enhance their meal for an additional £1.95, which includes a drink and an ice lolly, with the regular price being £5.45.

In conjunction with the meal deal, Joseph Holt has launched a fundraising campaign with a target of £20,000 for the charity Meals & More. This organisation provides free nutritious meals and activities for children during the school holidays when free school meals are not available.

Jane Kershaw, a director at Joseph Holt, emphasised the importance of community, stating that the initiative aims to alleviate the sadness of children going hungry during the holidays. The brewery is committed to supporting local areas and families.

Kerri Frost from Meals & More expressed gratitude for the partnership, highlighting that the funds raised will help combat food insecurity among children during the summer months. The campaign encourages community involvement through various fundraising activities across the brewery's pubs.

title: The iconic Seven Sisters in Rochdale where pigeons outnumber people
excerpt: Hundreds of the flats in the tower blocks are slowly being emptied, and one man tells the MEN he's glad to have left
content_html:
Hundreds of the flats in the tower blocks are slowly being emptied, and one man tells the MEN he's glad to have left

It’s a hot summer’s day at Seven Sisters, but Tony Warner looks over a lifeless stretch of concrete that connects the iconic Rochdale tower blocks. The seven 1960s behemoths, actually named College Bank, used to be a hive of activity in their heyday. Now almost all of the 761 flats are empty. Tony’s old flat is empty now too. The 60-year-old was one of 229 who received a letter from landlord Rochdale Boroughwide Housing (RBH) back in October saying they would all be moving out. He returned to his old stomping ground to talk to the Manchester Evening News about his life after leaving his home of 19 years. And Tony is glad he left. title: Heywood Foster Family Uses Football to Build Bonds with Young People
excerpt: A Heywood couple, Lauren and Danny Sullivan, have shared how their passion for football has fostered a sense of belonging among the children in their care. Since 2012, they have been providing a nurturing environment for young people, using the sport to strengthen relationships and ease transitions into family life.
content_html:
Lauren and Danny Sullivan, a foster family from Heywood, have highlighted the role of football in creating strong connections with the young people they care for. Since beginning their fostering journey with Rochdale Borough Council in 2012, the couple has welcomed five boys aged between 12 and 21 into their home, alongside their nine-year-old son, Daniel.

Football has become an integral part of their daily routine, with the family engaging in coaching, training, and weekend matches. Lauren expressed joy in seeing the smiles on the children's faces as they play together in their garden after school or college.

The Sullivans have found that football not only fosters relationships but also helps children adjust to their new environment. They recalled a memorable Champions League final party held to welcome their first long-term foster child, which helped ease his transition into the family.

Danny, who works as a football coach and scout, has supported one of the 17-year-olds in their care to gain coaching qualifications and become a grassroots coach. This mentorship has created a strong bond between them, allowing for discussions about coaching techniques and personal development.

Another young person, who initially spoke only French, quickly integrated into the family through football. Lauren noted that the shared activity helped break down language barriers, allowing him to connect with the other boys immediately.

The couple hopes to inspire others to consider fostering, emphasising the positive impact that love and support can have on young people in the care system. Councillor Rachel Massey from Rochdale Borough Council praised their efforts, highlighting the significant difference foster carers make in providing stability and a sense of belonging.

title: Bev Craig Unveils Transport Plans for Greater Manchester Mayoral Campaign
excerpt: Labour candidate Bev Craig has announced her transport proposals, including a fare freeze and expansion of the Bee Network, aimed at improving public transport across Greater Manchester.
content_html:
Bev Craig, the Labour candidate for Mayor of Greater Manchester, has revealed her plans to enhance public transport in the region. During the launch of her campaign in Stockport, she outlined a series of initiatives designed to benefit residents in areas including Rochdale, Bury, and Bolton.

Among her key proposals is the expansion of the Bee Network, which would introduce new bus routes and support the next phase of Metrolink expansion to Stockport. Craig also aims to integrate more rail services into the network by extending the contactless payment system across trains.

Craig has committed to freezing the current £2 bus fare cap until 2027, while also widening access to free travel. This includes free bus passes for individuals aged 11 to 18, as well as for elderly and disabled passengers. Labour estimates that families with two secondary school children could save approximately £20 weekly under these plans.

In addition to fare-related initiatives, Craig's transport package includes a dedicated fund for pothole repairs across Greater Manchester, aimed at improving road conditions and reducing vehicle damage.

Further details on the funding of these proposals will be included in the Labour party's upcoming mayoral manifesto. Craig emphasised her commitment to making decisions that benefit the financial well-being of residents throughout the city region.
